crime.value: Murder by poison in coffee shop; method: poisoned sugar packet; question: who among the limited suspects had means and motive
crime.quote: David suggested jokingly that he could be the killer, and 'do the deed' via poison in a sugar packet.

victim.value: Laz, a designer/writer who stole ideas and business from competitors, made enemies through harsh criticism and plagiarism
victim.quote: Laz was stealing ideas and business from David, who needed the extra funds for his wife, who is currently in the hospital.

closed_world.value: Small coffee shop during rainstorm with limited customers - older couple, writer, waitress, and a few others
closed_world.quote: There were only a few; an older couple sat two tables away to his left, engaged in a subdued, yet heated discussion.

culprit_and_motive.value: David killed Laz for financial reasons - Laz was stealing his design ideas and David needed money for his hospitalized wife
culprit_and_motive.quote: Money. Laz was stealing ideas and business from David, who needed the extra funds for his wife

resolution.value: Patrick the investigator solves the case through deductive reasoning and David confesses to bringing poisoned sugar packet
resolution.quote: David was arrested for the murder, and confessed that he'd brought the poison in a sugar packet.

investigator.value: Patrick, apparently an official investigator who interviews suspects and pieces together the evidence methodically
investigator.quote: Patrick leaned back. 'Interesting. Thanks, Val. Please send Beth to me.'

suspect_architecture.value: Four suspects within Bookrix writing community: Val (critical reviews motive), Rick (contest jealousy), Beth (financial exploitation), David (business theft/wife's medical needs)
suspect_architecture.quote: Rick's been bumped from the top ten at the last moment by Laz's books in the last three contests

red_herring.value: Multiple suspects presented with apparent motives (Val's criticism frustration, Rick's contest jealousy, Beth's cover art debt) to misdirect from true killer David
red_herring.quote: What would drive Val to kill Laz?

clue_fairness.value: David's prophetic joke comment about being killer via poison sugar packet shown to reader before reveal, along with his quiet behavior pattern
clue_fairness.quote: David suggested jokingly that he could be the killer, and 'do the deed' via poison in a sugar packet

social_world.value: Bookrix online writing community with contest rankings, critique relationships, cover art business, and competitive creative economy dynamics
social_world.quote: You've seen his posts on Bookrix. Rick's been bumped from the top ten at the last moment

alibi.value: David maintains quiet, unobtrusive presence during coffee shop gathering, avoiding direct contact with victim's cup to prevent fingerprint evidence
alibi.quote: David did not touch Laz's cup, keeping his fingerprints from the murder weapon, so to speak

reveal_mechanic.value: Pre-planned poison delivery via sugar packet method, with David's own manuscript page revealing the solution as meta-fictional element
reveal_mechanic.quote: David was arrested for the murder, and confessed that he'd brought the poison in a sugar packet

media_and_audience.value: Bookrix community context frames crime as creative competition gone wrong, with meta-fictional manuscript revealing solution
media_and_audience.quote: The title, The Coffee Shop Murder, glowed in the light of the lamp hanging above the door

investigator_wound.value: Patrick loses a friend (Laz) and must solve case involving his own creative community, creating personal investment and loss
investigator_wound.quote: I'm sorry you lost a friend, but am glad this case is solved

_meta.title: ebook david stacey doss the coffee shop murder
